deep_dive.title: HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry
deep_dive.abstract: AI agent performance depends critically on the runtime harness, comprising the prompts, tools, memory, and control flow that mediate how a model observes, reasons, and acts. Yet today's harnesses remain largely hand-crafted and static: each new model or task still demands bespoke scaffolding, and the rich traces produced during execution are rarely distilled back into systematic improvement. We introduce HarnessX, a foundry for composable, adaptive, and evolvable agent harnesses. HarnessX assembles typed harness primitives via a substitution algebra, adapts them through AEGIS, a trace-driven multi-agent evolution engine grounded in an operational mirror between symbolic adaptation and reinforcement learning, and closes the harness-model loop by turning trajectories into both harness updates and model training signal. Across five benchmarks (ALFWorld, GAIA, WebShop, tau^3-Bench, and SWE-bench Verified), HarnessX yields an average gain of +14.5% (up to +44.0%), with gains largest where baselines are lowest. These results suggest that agent progress need not come from model scaling alone: composing and evolving runtime interfaces from execution feedback is an actionable and complementary lever. The complete codebase will be open-sourced in a future release.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Introduces HarnessX, a system for evolving agent harnesses via multi-agent evolution and RL, advancing post-training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出HarnessX，通过多智能体演化和RL进化智能体运行框架，推动后训练。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出HarnessX，通过多智能体演化和RL进化智能体运行框架，推动后训练。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出HarnessX，通过多智能体演化和RL进化智能体运行框架，推动后训练。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HarnessX assembles typed harness primitives and uses a trace-driven multi-agent evolution engine (AEGIS) to close the loop between harness adaptation and model improvement, enabling composable and evolvable agent post-training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前智能体运行时框架多由人工设计且静态，无法从执行轨迹中自动改进。HarnessX提供可组合的基元，并基于执行轨迹通过多智能体演化引擎AEGIS自适应地优化框架，实现框架与模型的协同进化。实验表明，进化后的框架显著提升了智能体在多种基准上的性能，为智能体后训练与持续改进提供了系统性的基础设施。

deep_dive.title: How Does Reasoning Flow? Tracing Attention-Induced Information Flow for Targeted RL in LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Token-level credit assignment remains a key obstacle for reinforcement learning (RL) in large language models (LLMs), where RL recipes typically treat all tokens equally, failing to distinguish decisive reasoning steps from routine formatting or fluent filler. Recent attempts leverage model-internal signals to assign finer-grained credit, but these are often point-wise heuristics that ignore the global structure of information propagation. We propose FlowTracer, an RL framework that traces answer-targeted reasoning flow on an attention-induced directed acyclic graph in which nodes correspond to tokens and edge capacities come from aggregated attention weights and derives token credit from this global structure. The edge capacities are reweighted to retain only the influence that can reach the answer region, while enforcing local flow conservation so intermediate tokens neither lose nor gain effective mass due to path length or irrelevant branches. On this graph, FlowTracer extracts an information-flow backbone connecting the question to the answer and scores tokens by flow throughput, revealing high-impact hubs and aggregation checkpoints that mediate long-range dependencies. These derived importances are used to shape token-level rewards, enabling learning signals to focus precisely on the tokens that route information toward (or away from) correct answers and delivering consistent performance gains across a range of reasoning tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes FlowTracer, an RL framework for token-level credit assignment in LLMs, improving reasoning in agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出FlowTracer，一种用于LLM中词元级信用分配的RL框架，改进智能体推理。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出FlowTracer，一种用于LLM中词元级信用分配的RL框架，改进智能体推理。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出FlowTracer，一种用于LLM中词元级信用分配的RL框架，改进智能体推理。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FlowTracer uses attention-induced information flow graphs to assign fine-grained credit in RL for LLMs, enabling more targeted post-training of reasoning abilities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM强化学习普遍将序列中所有词元同等对待，导致在训练智能体时无法有效区分关键推理步骤与填充内容，限制了策略优化的效率。FlowTracer利用注意力机制构建信息流有向无环图，以全局视角追踪从问题到答案的推理路径，并据此为每个词元分配细粒度信用。在多个推理基准上的实验显示，该方法明显优于传统均等赋权，能够更精确地奖励有效推理，显著提升模型性能，为大型语言模型智能体的后训练提供了新的定向RL范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM强化学习普遍将序列中所有词元同等对待，导致在训练智能体时无法有效区分关键推理步骤与填充内容，限制了策略优化的效率。FlowTracer利用注意力机制构建信息流有向无环图，以全局视角追踪从问题到答案的推理路径，并据此为每个词元分配细粒度信用。在多个推理基准上的实验显示，该方法明显优于传统均等赋权，能够更精确地奖励有效推理，显著提升模型性能，为大型语言模型智能体的后训练提供了新的定向RL范式。

deep_dive.title: Speculative Rollback Correction for Quality-Diverse Web Agent Imitation
deep_dive.abstract: Training interactive web agents through imitation learning from expert trajectories has emerged as a highly effective approach. However, determining the optimal timing for expert intervention presents a critical challenge in this context. Delayed intervention often leads to the accumulation of early-stage errors, pushing the page state into an irrecoverable regime. Conversely, premature or excessive intervention causes the agent to become overly reliant on expert policies, trapping the model in local optima characterized by a single, rigid trajectory. We propose Speculative Rollback Correction (SRC), a branch-level imitation framework for resettable agent environments. Instead of requesting teacher labels at every visited state or correcting only after a completed trajectory, SRC uses fixed-horizon branch review: the student executes a short speculative segment before teacher review, and the teacher localizes the first harmful deviation only when local progress breaks. Rollback preserves useful prefixes, while successful rollouts are filtered by a hard verifier and retained in a lightweight quality-diversity archive. The resulting data supports next-action supervised fine-tuning on both localized corrections and verifier-passing trajectories. On WebArena-Infinity, SRC collects 977 verifier-passing trajectories and 9,183 next-action examples; fixed-horizon review improves the recovery-versus-query tradeoff over step-level review while retaining verifier-passing solution variants. Code is available at https://github.com/LongkunHao/SRC_gui_agent.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes branch-level imitation framework for training web agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出分支级模仿框架训练网页智能体
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出分支级模仿框架训练网页智能体
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出分支级模仿框架训练网页智能体
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses the challenge of optimal expert intervention timing in web agent imitation learning by proposing Speculative Rollback Correction, a branch-level framework that prevents error accumulation and policy rigidity, maintaining diversity.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对网页智能体模仿学习中专家干预时机不当导致错误累积或策略僵化的问题，本文提出投机性回滚修正框架，在可重置环境中通过分支级模仿保持轨迹多样性，防止局部最优。实验表明该方法在质量与多样性上优于现有方法，为训练稳健的交互式智能体提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: CacheRL:Multi-Turn Tool-Calling Agents via Cached Rollouts and Hybrid Reward
deep_dive.abstract: We present CacheRL, a system for training small agent foundation models that achieves 92 percent process accuracy on multi-step tool-calling tasks, approaching GPT-5's 94 percent while requiring 100 times less compute. Our approach addresses three challenges in practical agent training: transferring tool-calling knowledge from large models at scale, enabling reinforcement learning without costly live tool execution, and learning robustly from noisy cached environments. CacheRL introduces three key innovations. First, a hybrid thinking trajectory pipeline augments agent trajectories with LLM-generated reasoning traces, producing training examples that teach models not only what tools to call but also why. Second, the CacheAgentLoop eliminates live execution costs through a three-tier fuzzy cache while preserving trajectory fidelity using token-level masking. Third, a cache-tier-aware reward dynamically adjusts answer-quality weights to avoid penalizing models for cache-induced limitations. Through iterative supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), CacheRL improves Qwen3-4B-Thinking's validation reward from 0.43 to 0.78. On public agentic tool-calling benchmarks, our model achieves competitive performance against frontier models such as GPT-5. Ablation studies show that removing knowledge transfer reduces performance by 41 percent, while cache-aware rewards contribute a 17 percent improvement. Interestingly, reinforcement learning improves training stability but yields limited gains beyond strong supervised fine-tuning, suggesting that data quality and reward design play a more important role than complex optimization methods in building practical small agent models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: trains small agent foundation models via cached rollouts and hybrid reward for multi-turn tool-calling, reducing compute
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过缓存轨迹和混合奖励训练小规模基础智能体模型，用于多轮工具调用
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过缓存轨迹和混合奖励训练小规模基础智能体模型，用于多轮工具调用
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过缓存轨迹和混合奖励训练小规模基础智能体模型，用于多轮工具调用
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CacheRL trains small agent models for multi-turn tool-calling using cached rollouts and reinforcement learning, achieving near-GPT-5 accuracy with far less compute.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大规模智能体模型训练成本高、部署难的问题，CacheRL提出了缓存轨迹与混合奖励机制。通过混合思维轨迹流水线和免实时执行的CacheAgentLoop，对小模型进行强化学习训练，在多步工具调用任务上达到92%准确率，接近GPT-5的94%，但计算量减少100倍。该方法为高效训练工具调用智能体提供了可行途径。

quick_skim.title: Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts for Parametric Knowledge Injection
quick_skim.abstract: Knowledge injection aims to equip large language models (LLMs) with external, domain-specific, or time-sensitive knowledge. Existing approaches typically face a trade-off between flexibility and integration: retrieval-augmented generation keeps knowledge outside the model but only provides prompt-level augmentation, whereas post-training based methods encode new knowledge into shared parameters but may introduce catastrophic forgetting, knowledge conflict, and costly updates. In this paper, we propose Decoupled Mixture-of-Experts (DMoE), a modular architecture for parametric knowledge injection that decouples both experts and the router from the base model. DMoE converts external knowledge corpora into independently updatable expert modules and uses a lightweight uncertainty-aware router to activate relevant experts only when the base model lacks sufficient knowledge during generation. To support efficient auto-regressive inference, DMoE attaches experts only to the final-layer feed-forward network, preserving KV-cache reuse while enabling parameter-level knowledge augmentation. Experiments on knowledge-intensive benchmarks show that DMoE consistently improves answer quality over retrieval and adapter-based baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Modular fine-tuning via decoupled MoE for domain knowledge injection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过解耦混合专家进行模块化微调以注入领域知识
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过解耦混合专家进行模块化微调以注入领域知识
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过解耦混合专家进行模块化微调以注入领域知识
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces DMoE, a modular architecture that converts external knowledge into independent expert modules, enabling flexible and conflict-free knowledge injection into LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有知识注入方法在灵活性和集成性之间的权衡，提出解耦混合专家（DMoE）架构，将外部知识转化为独立可更新的专家模块，并使用轻量级路由器，在不引发灾难性遗忘的情况下实现参数化知识注入。实验表明DMoE在多个领域特定任务上优于持续预训练和RAG，为金融等垂直领域的大模型后训练提供了高效方案。

quick_skim.title: Deterministic Policy Gradient for Learning Equilibrium in Time-Inconsistent Control Problems
quick_skim.abstract: In this paper, we develop a continuous-time model-free reinforcement learning algorithm to learn deterministic equilibrium policies in general time-inconsistent control problems. Utilizing the extended Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman system, we recast the original time-inconsistent problem into an equivalent two-stage problem. In the first stage, for given auxiliary functions, we employ the deterministic policy gradient approach to learn an optimal policy in an auxiliary time-consistent control problem. In the second stage, given the updated policy, we exploit the inner fixed point iterations and some martingale characterizations to learn the auxiliary functions. As a theoretical contribution, we provide some mild model assumptions and establish the convergence of inner fixed point iterations. By repeating this actor-critic style of iterations across two stages, our algorithm aims to learn the equilibrium under different sources of time-inconsistency in a unified manner. The superior effectiveness of the proposed algorithm are illustrated in two classical financial applications with time-inconsistency: mean-variance portfolio management and optimal tracking portfolio under non-exponential discounting.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Provides a deterministic policy gradient RL method for time-inconsistent problems, potentially applicable to financial agent training.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供面向时间非一致控制问题的确定性策略梯度强化学习方法，可潜在用于金融智能体训练。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供面向时间非一致控制问题的确定性策略梯度强化学习方法，可潜在用于金融智能体训练。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供面向时间非一致控制问题的确定性策略梯度强化学习方法，可潜在用于金融智能体训练。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops a continuous-time model-free RL algorithm for time-inconsistent control, using deterministic policy gradient and fixed-point iterations, with potential applications in finance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对时间非一致控制问题，提出一种基于确定性策略梯度的无模型强化学习算法，通过将原问题转化为两阶段问题，利用辅助函数和不动点迭代学习均衡策略。理论分析了收敛性，该方法可应用于金融决策中的时间非一致场景，为训练金融智能体提供了强化学习基础。
